data.epi_content:
"Ah, my head hurts."

When I opened my eyes, I was in a room I remembered entering before.

Vivian's room was exactly as I remembered it, or rather, it looked exactly the same as when I had entered it last time.

"Erica, have you calmed down a bit...?"

Seeing that I had opened my eyes, Vivian approached me and looked me over, trying to make sure I was really okay.

Her gaze felt unpleasant, like a worm wriggling and scanning over my body.

"Calmed down? What nonsense are you talking about after dragging me here by force?"

I was lying on the bed.

When I tried to get up, using my arms to lift my body, I realized my arm, which had a straight cut from a knife, was tied to the headboard with rope.

The rope was tied so tightly that even when I moved my arms around making the headboard shake, it still firmly bound my arm.

"Ah, damn it."

Vivian hesitated for a moment when she heard my curse.

Ah, is this the first time she's heard me swear?

"......"

"I'm sorry for tying you up. But your wound was so severe.

I called Evan, so he'll do something about it."

"This isn't a joke. Untie me right now."

When I frowned and said that, Vivian obediently untied the rope, probably thinking she could overpower me anyway.

Then, when Vivian sat down in a chair to drink her tea, I tried to take out the pistol I had been carrying at my waist to shoot myself in the head.

It felt empty.

There was nothing inside.

"Perhaps you're looking for this?"

The pistol, with all the bullets removed from the chamber, was placed next to a bowl full of what looked like baked snacks. Vivian had it.

I didn't like how the ivory grip and gold decorations unnecessarily gleamed in the room's light.

"First kidnapping, then tying me up, and now you've searched my person and taken away my precious possession."

"Someone who was speaking normally and even getting angry just moments ago.

Someone who seemed so full of self-esteem and confidence, who looked so dignified and impressive—what would you think if you saw them twisted in such a strange way?"

Saying that, Vivian brought me a lukewarm cup of tea that wasn't even steaming and a cookie filled with chocolate chips.

I took them but didn't feel like eating, so I just placed them on the bed.

"What would I think?

Was it so difficult to just think 'they're having a hard time' and let it go?"

"It is difficult, especially when class ended long ago, and there's no reason for anyone to harm you, yet your arm has a very deep knife wound in a straight line."

"I could have been drinking tea, accidentally broke the cup, and got cut."

Even as I said it, I couldn't help but laugh at how ridiculous it sounded.

But I continued to make these absurd excuses to try to get out of this room.

Because it seemed like Vivian wasn't going to let me leave.

"Do you think that's a believable excuse?!"
data.epi_content2:
Vivian, seemingly angry at my words, opened her eyes wide and shouted at me.

Memories of being pinned down before, unable to move, flashed through my mind, and I instinctively shrank back.

"If it's so precious to you, you could just carry it as a decoration!

All six bullets were loaded! With the hammer cocked, ready to fire with just a pull of the trigger!"

Vivian said, lightly hitting the table.

Thanks to that, even though I tried to let her words go in one ear and out the other, they forcefully lodged themselves in my mind.

"...Can't you just leave me alone?"

Whether it's Evan, you, or Lydia, I wish you would all just leave me be.

"Could you please return my pistol?"

Vivian pushed my pistol aside, indicating refusal.

Looking carefully, I could see the bullets had already been scattered on the floor.

She was looking at me with a confused yet worried expression.

The truth is, this problem would be solved if you all just stopped caring about me.

If Lydia didn't care about me and just went about her business at the Academy without causing any trouble, there would be no need for a hole in my forehead.

Evan is good for not caring about me, at least after our fight.

This lady still has tender, positive feelings for Evan, but I don't want to use my mental energy.

I can't keep having all sorts of injuries buried in my heart while only coming out at times like this.

I wonder if she would listen better if I burned my face instead of just cutting my arm.

And Vivian. I wish this awkward girl would just leave me alone.

I don't want to feel jealous being next to you. I don't want to envy you, and I don't want to feel miserable by your side.

I don't want to feel my inadequacies.

Yet I feel grateful that you show me affection.

Even though I pushed you away, when I see you offering me cookies and tea leaves and talking to me, I just feel like a bug.

The Crown Prince is the best.

Although he completely pushed me into the abyss, he doesn't even care as long as I don't do something crazy like openly killing a student in the Academy under imperial jurisdiction.

Haha, these are words I can't say out loud.

Or maybe I could after dying a few more times.

When I first fell here and woke up as this lady, I never dreamed of things like this.

Cursing, causing riots, killing people, going berserk.

The joy of regaining my lost limbs was overshadowed by this lady's obsessive need to maintain her dignity.

"Vivian, that's all I have left now.

I have nothing else left, so will you please return it to me?"

I say to Vivian with a smile.

"...Miss, I lived in the same house with someone who made that kind of expression."

Vivian says with a somewhat dark expression, as if about to share a deep story.

But although I've never heard it directly, it's just something I've known for a long time.

It might sound dismissive, but even after your father died, you still had people who cared for and cherished you.

Just because you lived alone doesn't mean you had people specifically targeting you for harassment, and even if you were harassed, you would have somehow overcome it.

Look at you now, with your outstanding magical abilities, beautiful appearance, and positive attitude that makes me ashamed to even be jealous...

As these thoughts surfaced, I shook my head once and asked again.
data.epi_content3:
"What's wrong with my expression, Vivian?"

When I asked that, Vivian kindly tried to explain.

"Like someone who has given up everything, and is now trying to give up the one last thing God has given them..."

When she mentioned the one last thing God had given, I started crying.

Just smiling while tears flowed down—it must have been a strange sight.

I, the lady, no, I, or am I the lady after all? No, I think I'm myself, but anyway, the distinction doesn't matter as I've said many times—I just burst into tears.

Though I didn't make sobbing sounds, my breathing became rapid, and it became difficult to suppress the sounds when inhaling and exhaling.

Tears ran down my cheeks to my chin, forming small droplets that couldn't bear the weight of the tears coming from above and simply fell.

"I gave up long ago. But someone. Someone won't let me give up."

My vision blurred for a moment.

I don't know if it's because my eyes were filled with tears or if my emotions made me dizzy.

I'm full of unknowns.

I don't know who caused me such pain, nor do I know what Vivian's so-called God wants from me.

I can only resent and curse, unable to properly express it out loud, just begging someone much stronger than me to return what's mine.

I got up from my seat, placed the tea and cookies Vivian had given me on the table in front of her, and sat down across from her.

Then, to soothe my burning throat, I drank a cup of tea and chewed on the cookies.

Well, I wouldn't say I ate ravenously.

After eating cookies and drinking tea for a while, filling my stomach, I spoke to Vivian as if just making casual complaints.

"Just leave me alone. Anyone, please.

Don't talk to me. Don't worry about me.

Don't look at me with eyes of pity. Don't show me affection.

Don't feel sorry for my past. Don't think about my family.

Just put me in a corner and occasionally remember that such a person existed, then forget about me in your mind and don't care about me."

Then, I threw off the white cloth covering my right arm and tore open the healing wound with my bare hand.

The skin twisted strangely as blood began to flow.

"Whatever I do, whether I starve to death on the street, or whether I kill someone.

Whatever I do, please don't care. Just leave me alone.

Don't pity me, don't worry about me."

With my left hand, which had just torn open the wound, I picked up the teacup and slammed it down on the table.

Again, there was no dramatic shattering sound.

Still, the glass split in half.

Vivian, with a face that looked like she was about to cry, grabbed my arm to stop me from moving.

She gripped so tightly that there was a slight cracking sound from my right hand, and my left hand twisted, sending signals to my brain that it was painful and would soon lose its function.

"If you happen to think of me occasionally, just spit on my grave and I'll be satisfied!"

With a flushed face full of anger yet somehow sad, Vivian grabbed me and threw me onto the bed.

Then she overpowered me and started tying me to the headboard again.

This time, both arms.

"So, please. Hic."

A hiccup came along with crying, or perhaps laughter.

"Leave me alone..."

I feel dizzy.
